sources_exist.note: Identity and all four deterministic dataset_inputs match the assigned manifest and derived dataset exactly, but the buy-multiple and owner-age URLs were inaccessible, while the other five URLs resolved.

figures_match_sources.note: Spectora supports the 1-in-3 AI-adoption claim and Angi supports fixed pricing and the $296-$424 range, but the cited WIN article contains none of the claimed Porch/Radian acquisition evidence, BizBuySell contains no home-inspection or 7-9x platform multiple, HomeInspecto does not establish a 2016 50% crossing, and Angi currently says $344 rather than the quoted $343.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Most declared values are bounded and directionally plausible under the prompt's inspector-specific role reconstruction, but inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[1].within_role_automatable_fraction is 0.25 despite its own stated plausible range of 0.10-0.20, and t50_years=3 depends on an unsupported historical crossing date.

rubric_consistent.note: The flat-fee capture and physical-presence logic follow the frozen rubric, but A and M rely on unsupported analog/multiple evidence. BORDERLINE: |S - conditional cut 4.00| = 0.0972 therefore heightens rather than cures the score-affecting sourcing defects. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, S is within the orchestrator's arithmetic tolerance and is not independently disqualifying.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift and no-proxy checks are internally candid, but the terminal-value discussion relies on uncited waiver-rate assertions and pricing_model_deflation attributes a 25-50% report-writing-time reduction to Spectora even though the audited Spectora page does not state that percentage.

source_audits.note: The WIN franchise article discusses industry growth, service diversification, technology, and WIN's franchise support. It does not mention Porch, Radian Capital, an acquisition streak, PE-backed add-ons, or establish a count of two active consolidators.
The cited page was inaccessible during full audit, so the claimed 2x-4x SDE, 3.2x-6.5x EBITDA, and 2.8x average SDE figures could not be verified.

Spectora's July 30, 2025 article states that one in three inspectors use AI tools in their workflows and that 58% plan near-term adoption; it also says 83% of AI-using inspectors use it for report writing.
The generic BizBuySell industry-multiples page does not contain a home-inspection category, the claimed 3.2x-6.5x home-inspection EBITDA range, or the asserted 7-9x PE platform exit range.

The vendor page states 91% current digital-reporting use versus 42% a decade earlier, but supplies no intervening observations and does not establish that adoption crossed 50% in 2016 or two years after the 42% observation.
The cited ASHI page was inaccessible during full audit, so the quoted age distribution, inferred 35% age-60-plus share, and machine-readable succession-shortage claim could not be verified.

Angi supports a $296-$424 range and says many inspectors charge a fixed price up to 2,000 square feet, but the current page gives a $344 average rather than the quoted $343 and does not support the asserted approximately 90%-plus residential-revenue share.

reasons: Replace inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://wini.com/franchise/future-of-home-inspection-industry/) with transaction-level evidence naming PE-backed platforms and add-ons in the last 24 months; the cited WIN article does not support the value of two.

Supply an accessible transaction or broker source for inputs.buy_mult.url (https://homeservicesbusinessvaluation.com/home-inspection-business-valuation/how-to-sell-a-home-inspection-business) and document the SDE-to-EBITDA normalization used for the small-firm buy multiple.

Replace inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.bizbuysell.com/learning-center/industry-valuation-multiples/) with a credible $10M-plus-EBITDA platform transaction or comparable; the generic page supports neither the home-inspection range nor the 7-9x assertion.

Re-derive inputs.historical_analogs.value and inputs.t50_years from dated adoption observations; https://homeinspecto.com/blog/digital-reporting-professional-insight provides only decade endpoints and cannot establish a 2016 50% crossing.

Provide an accessible source for inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://www.homeinspector.org/engaging-the-next-generation-of-home-inspectors-opportunity-urgency-and-a-path-forward) and evidence supporting both the 35% age-60-plus estimate and succession flag.

Correct inputs.pricing_structure.figure_quoted and substantiate the claimed approximately 90%-plus residential revenue mix; https://www.angi.com/articles/how-much-does-home-inspection-cost.htm currently reports a $344 average and does not quantify revenue mix.

Reconcile inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[1].within_role_automatable_fraction with its declared range: 0.25 exceeds the stated 0.10-0.20 bound; provide a supported rationale and internally consistent range for the rerun.
Correct cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation or cite evidence for its claimed 25-50% report-writing-time reduction; the audited Spectora URL does not state that percentage.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, S
